Title: NbHDR, A Host Protein Involved in the MEP Pathway, Interacts With Bamboo Mosaic Virus Replicase and Enhances Viral Accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gibberellic acid (context); BaMV (+)RNA accumulation/viral RNA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: GA regulates plant growth; in this study NbHDR effect on BaMV replication is largely independent of GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast-associated MEP pathway context; NA for viral RNA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastidial MEP pathway upstream of GGPP and GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream step in core MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HDR/IspH family; chloroplast transit peptide-containing plastid enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Slightly induced by BaMV infection at 5 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; slight increase in mRNA and protein upon BaMV infection
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBPP to the isoprenoid precursors IPP and DMAPP in the plastidial MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BaMV infection; GA application; VIGS knockdown; transient overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes BaMV accumulation and enhances initiation of viral (+)RNA synthesis; effect is not rescued by exogenous GA after NbHDR silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; co-immunoprecipitation; pull-down assay; yeast two-hybrid; VIGS knockdown; transient overexpression; protoplast infection; immunodepletion; in vitro RdRp assay with recombinant protein; metabolite-related GA rescue test
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbHDR was identified from BaMV replicase complexes by IP-MS and physically interacts with the replicase RdRp domain. 2. NbHDR knockdown strongly reduced BaMV genomic RNA and coat protein accumulation in leaves and protoplasts, while overexpression increased viral accumulation. 3. Recombinant NbHDR enhanced initiation of BaMV (+)RNA synthesis in vitro, and GA could not rescue BaMV reduction caused by NbHDR silencing, indicating a GA-independent direct role in replication.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: dual 35S promoter for BaMV clone expression; transient expression vector promoter not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused chlorosis/photobleaching, reflecting essential metabolic role in chlorophyll/isoprenoid-related physiology
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS proteomics (IP-MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host-factor target for engineering BaMV resistance, but suppression may incur strong physiological penalties due to essential MEP-pathway function; precise allele editing or disruption of virus-interaction interface may be preferable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes a catalytically inactive mutant still enhanced BaMV accumulation, though less strongly, suggesting dual structural and metabolic roles; mutant data were in supplementary figures

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Growth-regulating hormone; in this system supports BaMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GA biosynthetic branch downstream of GGPP
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: key committed/early step in GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; silenced by VIGS in infection assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BaMV infection; exogenous GA treatment; VIGS knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduced BaMV accumulation, and exogenous GA restored viral accumulation to basal levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; RT-qPCR; GA rescue experiment; western blot of BaMV coat protein
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbKS knockdown significantly reduced BaMV accumulation. 2. Exogenous GA rescued the reduced BaMV accumulation in NbKS-silenced plants. 3. NbKS therefore affects BaMV primarily through GA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely perturbs GA-dependent growth processes when suppressed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating this gene for virus resistance would likely disrupt GA-mediated growth and is therefore biologically costly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a comparative core pathway gene in the Results to distinguish GA-dependent versus GA-independent effects
